name: Lena Horne
description: American singer, actress, dancer and activist (1917–2010)
abstract: Lena Mary Calhoun Horne (June 30, 1917 – May 9, 2010) was an American singer, actress, dancer and civil rights activist. Horne's career spanned more than seventy years and covered film, television and theater.

works.t: Porgy and Bess: Act I, Scene 1. Summertime

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where my head was. Or, there was another young woman out at the time. I can’t remember her name right now. But she was – these two were examples of what I wanted, which was really classy black women performing... to be another Lena Horne.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mona Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: acted as a business advisor to Count Basie as well as music contractor for Lena Horne, Quincy Jones and Sammy Davis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mona Hinton interviewed by Milt Fillius and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Did you -- so in that situation did you meet Lena Horne and -- [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Lena Horne evidently needed an arranger and music conductor and Ellington and Strayhorn who were very friendly with her suggested me. So anyway, I became, I went with, she came to my studio and we, she, I played something with her and so forth and she seemed to like it and all. Then we got together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Martin was on that great eight piece band that backed Lena Horn when I was playing with Lena Horne... I made most of the arrangements that were recorded, and I remember distinctly I had a couple of arrangements there that he didn't conduct them

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Louie Bellson has been a saint in my life. He helped me at the age of eighteen to become Lena Horne’s drummer... I was back in Miami Beach playing as Lena Horne’s drummer with Alan King.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995
